Melbourne Home Loans

FHA loans in Melbourne, Florida

Melbourne anchors the Space Coast's aerospace, defense, and tech economy, home to major employers and a charming historic downtown alongside newer communities like Suntree. That base of stable, well-paying jobs plus an affordable market makes Melbourne a strong FHA city — the statewide loan limit covers the typical home, and 3.5% down works for first-time buyers and relocating professionals alike.

Brevard County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Melbourne

With the 2026 FHA limit of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Melbourne home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). In Melbourne's affordable market, that covers the large majority of homes for sale. A few Florida counties are set higher, so confirm Brevard County's exact figure with HUD.

FHA and the Space Coast's tech economy

Melbourne's aerospace and tech employers give many buyers the steady income FHA underwriters favor, and local prices leave plenty of room under the loan limit — a great setup for first-time buyers and relocating workers. Newer Suntree-area homes pass the FHA appraisal easily, while historic downtown homes that need updates can use an FHA 203(k) renovation loan.

Down payment help for Melbourne buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Melbourne buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Brevard County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Melbourne

We help buyers throughout Melbourne and Brevard County, including Downtown Melbourne, Suntree, and West Melbourne. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Melbourne (Brevard County) for 2026?+
Brevard County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers most Melbourne homes. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
How much home can I buy with FHA in Melbourne?+
With the $541,287 limit and 3.5% down, you can finance a home up to roughly $560,000 — well above typical Melbourne prices.
Is FHA good for relocating Space Coast workers?+
Yes — stable aerospace and tech employment plus FHA's low 3.5% down make it a strong fit for professionals moving to the area.
How much down payment do I need in Melbourne?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of Brevard County?+
Yes — Melbourne, Palm Bay, Titusville, Cocoa, and surrounding Space Coast communities.
